JOB-1396.summary: The merchant New Booking notification email renders its View Appointment CTA as `http:///appointments/{id}` — missing hostname — so every click hits Google's Redirect Notice instead of the appointment. Affects both public-booking and admin-created appointments, breaking the inbox-to-calendar jump for every new booking. Urgent, 1pt; PR #94 open, bounced from In Review back to In Progress.

JOB-722.summary: The inline "Add New Address" flow in the New Appointment panel is broken again after JOB-471 shipped it in March — admins can't add a client address without leaving the panel, and there's no Google Places autocomplete to capture lat/lng. That missing lat/lng also blocks the JOB-720 admin-side service radius check for mobile appointments.

JOB-569.summary: Reworks the admin schedule panel so calendar clicks pre-fill only date/time and default the provider to "Any Available," which triggers round-robin auto-assignment via findNextAvailableProvider(); picking a specific staffer marks the session as "requested" so cancellations notify the customer. Includes solo-practitioner shortcut, qualification auto-switch, and multi-service eligibility filtering — critical for phone bookings where speed and correct assignment_method drive downstream cancellation behavior.

JOB-525.summary: Builds the post-booking confirmation screen showing booking details, add-to-calendar links (Google, Outlook, Apple, .ics), and token-based Manage/Book Again buttons — including staggered group support that renders both batch times. Closes the customer booking flow so customers get a clear receipt without needing to log in.

JOB-1420.summary: Umbrella tracking the MVP access work (partner outreach, API decisions, prospect-list build) that gates every per-platform scheduling integration for SmartRoute, with Vagaro as the locked MVP target. Owns decisions like Vagaro vs Booksy write-API access and Square/Acuity outbound feasibility; per-platform build work lives in sibling epic JOB-1543.

JOB-1552.summary: Cold-outreach decision to pick between Vagaro and Booksy for write/create-appointment API access so SmartRoute can push bookings into the merchant's calendar and POS. Vagaro's public API is confirmed read-only and Booksy's partner door is locked, so if neither replies by 2026-07-22 the fallback is to promote Square or Acuity from post-MVP to MVP.

JOB-1434.summary: Strips the consumer-facing Stripe Link "Save my information for faster checkout" section (email, mobile, disclaimer) from the merchant Add Card side panel opened during the New Appointment flow. Merchants were filling in their own contact info or stalling because it was unclear whose account was being created; card fields and save behavior stay intact.

JOB-1391.summary: The Profile and security view renders at /settings while /settings/profile shows an empty stub, so the left-nav Settings link never reaches the canonical URL. Fix requires redirecting /settings to /settings/profile, moving the full view to the canonical route, and deleting the placeholder Personal information card.

JOB-1463.summary: The /billing UpgradeModal path creates a Stripe subscription with default_incomplete but never confirms the PaymentIntent: the server omits `latest_invoice.confirmation_secret` from the expand (returning clientSecret null on API 2025-11-17.clover), and the client calls onSuccess without running stripe.confirmCardPayment. Result: trial-expired users appear to upgrade successfully, aren't charged, and the "Trial expired" badge never clears until Stripe auto-cancels ~23h later. Fix mirrors the working PaymentForm confirmation flow.

JOB-511.summary: Adds a Stripe Connect step to business onboarding so operators can link an existing Stripe account or create one inline, storing the connected account ID for later charges. This is the prerequisite for JOB-510's upfront payment collection at booking — without it, no business can take card payments through SmartRoute.

JOB-700.summary: Builds the admin schedule panel's card-on-file UI (CardOnFileRow with 5 states, AdminCardModal via Stripe Elements + SetupIntent, and useScheduleSubmission wiring) on top of the already-shipped server enforcement that rejects cardless bookings. Without this, admins hit a 400 missing_payment_method with no way to collect a card in-flow, blocking the mandatory-card booking launch.

JOB-458.summary: Codify a single settings-page UI standard (breadcrumbs, typography, section cards, tabs, inputs, buttons, no stats cards) in settings-ui-standard.md and retrofit every existing settings page to match it. Eliminates the per-page styling drift that's making new settings work slow and inconsistent, and gives future pages a copy-paste template.

JOB-553.summary: Replaces the naive first-match team-member picker in the public booking API with a shared round-robin engine that filters by is_active, rota/shifts, location assignment, service qualification, conflicts, and drive time, then rotates fairly across eligible providers. Also fixes a live bug where inactive team members can be auto-assigned, and becomes the shared assignment function used by the admin schedule panel (JOB-569) and provider-cancelled reassignment (JOB-552).

JOB-747.summary: On fresh Calendar load in Day/Week view, today's appointments render briefly in the team-member columns then vanish, leaving admins staring at empty columns despite having scheduled work. Suspected hydration race where a client effect (likely selectedTeamMembers/selectedPlaces reset or an empty-state guard in useVisibleTeamMembers/CalendarRenderer) filters the appointment set to empty right after mount. Reopened twice already — same flash-family pattern as JOB-299/455/496.

JOB-755.summary: Stripe, marketing constants, and in-app billing each declare different prices ($29/$49/$79 vs $49/$119/$249), so customers get charged one amount and shown another with phantom per-member overages and a 1.5x multiplier. This ticket collapses all three sources onto the v3 four-tier structure (Solo/Team/Business/Growth + custom Enterprise), redefines "staff" as active-in-last-30-days, and migrates existing customers without billing surprises. Blocked, back In Progress after being bounced from Done.

JOB-762.summary: Remove the Stripe SetupIntent friction at merchant signup so trials match Acuity/Fresha/Square: no card for 14 days, Solo-tier caps to prevent abuse, and a clickable countdown pill that routes to /billing. On day 15 the account enters hard-freeze read-only (dashboard viewable, edits and public booking page blocked with a "contact merchant" message) until card + tier are both captured. Currently blocked and back In Progress after failing review.

JOB-1354.summary: Clicking Today in the Day/Week calendar returns to today's date but scrolls the grid to 12:00 AM instead of the current time, forcing admins to manually scroll to business hours on every click. Same root cause as JOB-505 (which fixed initial load) but for the Today button entry point.

JOB-1412.summary: In the New Appointment side panel, the Location dropdown renders detached from its input on first open (floating mid-panel over the Clients section) and overflows the panel's right edge at narrow widths. Scrolling re-anchors it correctly, so the fix is portal/positioning logic — not menu state. Makes location selection feel broken and unreliable at collapsed sidebar widths.

JOB-1454.summary: CI's E2E job hadn't actually run for months due to Verify/dotenvx breakages; now that infra is fixed, the ~37 specs have rotted (stale selectors, drifted seed/auth, timing) and the first real run hung 45+ min before cancellation. Triage each spec as regression vs stale vs local-only, get a clean green run, then drop retries from 2 and optionally carve a smoke subset so E2E can become a trustworthy PR gate again.

JOB-1483.summary: Sends a transactional email to the customer the moment their booking is marked no-show (manual or auto path, deduped), explaining whether the no-show fee was charged and offering a rebook CTA. Closes the silent-charge gap that today generates support tickets from confused customers. Follow-up to JOB-503, inherits the JOB-1481 template family.

JOB-1505.summary: The "You're Booked" confirmation screen shows the internal mode label "Mobile" alone with no address, leaving customers without a mappable destination. Fix renders LOCATION as mode/venue prefix plus the full street address including ZIP (e.g. `Mobile · 2047 Westcreek Ln, Houston, TX 77027`), so customers can see both what kind of appointment it is and where to go.

JOB-1507.summary: Strips the SR-XXXXXX confirmation code from the You're Booked in-app screen and the Booking Confirmed / reschedule / cancel emails, since customers manage bookings via the magic-link token, not by quoting a number. Purely a noise-reduction cleanup; the .ics calendar surface is handled separately in JOB-1522.

JOB-1509.summary: Manage Booking and Book Again CTAs in the customer Booking Confirmed email render dead links, stranding customers who can't reschedule, cancel, or rebook from the email. Likely shares a URL-builder root cause with JOB-1396 (merchant email's broken View Appointment link resolving to http:///), so the fix must land in the shared helper and be applied across all customer transactional emails under JOB-503.

JOB-749.summary: Register the Twilio 10DLC number (832) 658-2799 with TCR under a Low Volume Standard brand and an Account Notification campaign, then wire onboarding consent capture and privacy-policy updates. Without it, US carriers will filter SmartRoute's booking confirmations, reminders, and reschedule alerts — blocking the SMS launch tied to JOB-610/697/698/616.

JOB-1484.summary: Code audit confirmed Stripe Connect routes card holds and no-show fee charges directly to the merchant's connected account with zero platform fee, but a live test-mode round-trip is still required to prove the flow end-to-end. Now unblocked since JOB-511 (Connect onboarding) shipped — remaining work is deploying test keys to staging, onboarding a fake merchant, and running the AC checklist covering charge landing in merchant balance, receipt descriptor, and payout.

JOB-1592.summary: Detect a merchant's current booking tool at signup and, if it's unsupported, surface a migration CTA with an affiliate link to Vagaro, Square, or Acuity. Captures merchants who would otherwise bounce, grows SmartRoute's roster on platforms it integrates with, and generates interim revenue while new integrations are in flight.

JOB-1553.summary: Manual sleuthing playbook plus a curated list of 50+ mobile-services merchants on Square Appointments and Acuity Scheduling, sourced via Yelp/Google Maps, Instagram/TikTok hashtags, Reddit threads, and booking-widget fingerprinting. Neither platform has a public merchant directory, so this prep work makes outbound ready to launch the day the Square (JOB-1590) or Acuity (JOB-1591) integrations ship, and doubles as the target list for the affiliate model (JOB-1592) or standalone-platform contingency (JOB-1595).

JOB-1593.summary: Email Squire, Mindbody, Setmore, SimplyBook.me, and Booker to confirm whether their partner-tier APIs support create-appointment writes for a SmartRoute integration. Results feed the JOB-1595 breaking-point chain: if all 5 decline alongside Vagaro/Booksy/Square/Acuity stalling, the standalone booking platform contingency activates.

JOB-1541.summary: Set up Klaviyo DKIM/SPF/DMARC so it can authenticate as support@smart-route.app alongside Resend, unblocking the TR-3 Day 51 re-engagement flow (JOB-1489) and future behavioral win-back campaigns. Default to shared-domain auth with Resend unless volume threatens transactional deliverability, in which case spin up a dedicated marketing subdomain.

JOB-1523.summary: Bookings made without a chosen provider (Any Available) emit calendar events whose description contains the literal string "Provider: undefined" on both the customer .ics download and the admin's synced event. Fix is to render the Provider line conditionally — include it only when a provider name exists, omit it entirely otherwise — and apply the same guard to reschedule/cancel updates that inherit the format.

JOB-738.summary: Wire Featurebase webhooks into SmartRoute's Next.js/Supabase/Resend stack to power two automated email flows: a 24-48h personalized follow-up to prospects who upvote roadmap items (warm-lead capture from Sabine's address), and a "we shipped your request" blast to all upvoters when a post is marked Shipped. Turns the public roadmap into both a qualified sales channel and a high-ROI retention touchpoint, sharing one webhook route, cron job, and Supabase schema across both flows.

JOB-1579.summary: Enable Stripe's native customer-receipt emails (successful charges, failed payments, refunds) via Dashboard and configure Branding (logo, accent, support email) so MVP ships tax-compliant receipts with zero code. Includes a runbook so the toggles can be flipped off cleanly when JOB-1578's custom-branded receipts take over post-MVP.

JOB-1402.summary: Full rebuild of the smart-route.app marketing site (homepage, pricing, contact, about, supporting pages) as a coherent set rather than page-by-page patches. Current site predates the mobile beauty/wellness ICP, v3 pricing tiers, and locked "Any Available is the flow" positioning. Must reuse the JOB-760 pricing tier component and keep the Featurebase roadmap subdomain untouched.

JOB-1554.summary: Founder-led design-partner outreach to ~50-100 Vagaro CC-Processing merchants: cold email through warmed inboxes, 15-min discovery calls, a pre-recorded video walkthrough, and a "founding customer at $X/mo at launch" verbal ask logged in Attio. Goal is 10+ verbal commits within 8 weeks to validate that the Vagaro integration build (JOB-1544) is worth Zarian's weeks of engineering time before it ships into a vacuum.

JOB-1285.summary: Stand up SmartRoute's cold-outreach infrastructure: buy 1-2 non-brand outreach domains (e.g. trysmartroute.com), configure SPF/DKIM/DMARC, create 2-3 Workspace inboxes per domain, and connect them to the shared Instantly Growth subscription in warmup+outreach mode. Protects the brand domain from blocklist burn and unlocks growth-motion cold sends after a 2-4 week warming cycle.

JOB-1269.summary: Publish SPF and DMARC TXT records for smart-route.app on Vercel DNS (DKIM already live), then register the domain in Google Postmaster Tools via a site-verification TXT. Unlocks Gmail deliverability monitoring and prevents outbound mail from being spam-flagged once SmartRoute starts sending customer email. Deferred behind JOB-1078 and the plaitqr.com auth work since SmartRoute has no imminent outbound mail.

JOB-1403.summary: Ship SmartRoute's brand across every customer-facing surface: favicon and PWA/Apple icons in the Next.js repo, an OG card for link previews, and branded sender avatars plus header/footer marks for Workspace and Resend transactional emails. Currently the browser tab, shared links, and outbound emails all render as unbranded, undermining trust. Mirrors the JOB-990 rollout previously done for PlaitQR.

JOB-756.summary: Sweep help docs, support macros, admin labels, legal pages, and non-pricing marketing site copy to purge leftover "Clinic" tier language that leaked from Stripe's original product description. SmartRoute doesn't serve clinical customers, so mixed naming risks misleading prospects into expecting HIPAA support. Complements parallel tickets covering pricing pages (JOB-760), transactional emails (JOB-874), and code-level tier IDs (JOB-755).

JOB-885.summary: Defines the full failed-renewal dunning policy: a 7-day soft grace with full access, then read-only mode (calendar viewable, no new bookings, public booking page paused) until Stripe Smart Retries exhaust around day 21 and the subscription cancels. Ships the subscription_status state machine, webhook + scheduled transitions, access enforcement, header pill/in-line UI cues, and the four-email sequence via Resend from billing@smart-route.app. Prevents silent revenue loss and double-booking while giving merchants a clear recovery path via card update.
